content:
Hi everyone,

I just wrote up a detailed post about my journey getting the Rust PWM abstractions and a new driver for the TH1520 (Lichee Pi 4A) upstreamed. It covers the "why" behind the abstractions and how to write PWM drivers.

Since this community gave so much great feedback, I wanted to share the final write up in case it's helpful for others starting their own drivers.

You can read it here: https://mwilczynski.dev/posts/bringing-rust-to-the-pwm-subsystem/
This topic was moved here from #General > Blog post: A deep dive on the new Rust PWM abstractions by Benno Lossin.
(feel free to move back if you think it fits better in general :)

I didn't do much, but thanks for the mention, and congratulations!

It is very nice to see the article remarking the fact that the driver gets to be 100% safe Rust -- that is the key of this whole endeavor with Rust for Linux.

I was about to say that we should probably move it to Announcements, but I see Benno did just that :)
